description: Figure 10 presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels with varying Al contents (15Al, 17Al, and 19Al at.%) subjected to selected aging treatments. The black curve shows the 15Al alloy aged at 550 °C for 1 h, the red curve shows the 17Al alloy aged at 575 °C for 1 h, and the blue curve shows the 19Al alloy aged at 550 °C for 6 h. Indexed diffraction peaks indicate the presence of α/D0₃ (diamonds), α-Mn (flowers), and β-Mn (triangles) phases across the 2θ range of 20° to 110°. The patterns reveal that both α-Mn and β-Mn diffraction peaks coexist in these selected conditions, indicating coprecipitation of α-Mn and β-Mn during aging prior to complete transformation of the α matrix to β-Mn. The 19Al alloy exhibits particularly prominent β-Mn peaks after prolonged aging, while the relative intensities and presence of α/D0₃ reflections vary with composition and aging parameters. These results support the conclusion that increasing Al content slows β-Mn precipitation kinetics and that both α-Mn and β-Mn phases precipitate simultaneously during the early-to-mid stages of aging in these ultra-high specific hardness steels.
